identifier: Proceedings of the IEEE, vol. 63, no. 9, pp. 1278-1308; doi:10.1109/PROC.1975.9939
summary: Tutorial survey of architectural mechanisms for protecting stored information that states the eight classic secure-design principles, including least privilege, fail-safe defaults, complete mediation, and separation of privilege. The principle of least privilege — every program and user operating with the fewest privileges necessary — as applied in access-control audits originates in this paper.
title: The Protection of Information in Computer Systems
